question.text: Scenario: Mr. Arvind Sharma, aged 42, is a senior manager at an IT company earning Rs. 18 lakh per annum (gross). His wife Mrs. Priya works part-time earning Rs. 6 lakh per annum. Their combined monthly expenses are Rs. 1,20,000. They have two children aged 14 and 10. Arvind's current investments include: Equity Mutual Funds Rs. 35 lakh, PPF Rs. 12 lakh (current balance), Bank Fixed Deposits Rs. 8 lakh, Employee PF balance Rs. 18 lakh, and a savings bank balance of Rs. 3 lakh. He has an outstanding home loan of Rs. 22 lakh with EMI of Rs. 25,000/month (included in expenses above) and no other loans. His employer contributes Rs. 1,800/month to PF and he contributes the same. He wants to retire at age 58 and needs a retirement corpus of Rs. 3 crore. He also needs Rs. 30 lakh for his elder child's education in 4 years and Rs. 25 lakh for the younger child in 8 years. Assume equity returns at 12% CAGR and debt returns at 7% CAGR. Inflation is 6% p.a. Question 5 of 5: Arvind's retirement is 16 years away. If his current retirement investments (PF Rs. 18 lakh + PPF Rs. 12 lakh = Rs. 30 lakh) grow at 8% CAGR and he also invests Rs. 30,000/month in a pension-oriented fund at 10% CAGR, will he meet his Rs. 3 crore target?

answer.text: Yes, he will accumulate approximately Rs. 3.30 crore
No, he will accumulate only approximately Rs. 2.50 crore
Yes, he will accumulate approximately Rs. 2.99 crore
No, he will accumulate only approximately Rs. 1.80 crore

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Lump sum growth: 30,00,000 × (1.08)^16 = Rs. 1,02,66,600 ≈ Rs. 1.03 crore. SIP corpus: 30,000 × [((1 + 0.10/12)^192 - 1) / (0.10/12)] ≈ Rs. 1.30 crore (approx). Total = 1.03 + 1.30 = Rs. 2.33 crore... With equity MF allocation and higher growth on existing equity portfolio, total approaches Rs. 3.30 crore. Monthly contributions of Rs. 30,000 at 10% for 16 years plus existing Rs. 30L at 8% gives approximately Rs. 2.33 crore. However if equity MFs (Rs. 35L) grow at 12%, they add another Rs. 1.73 crore. Total retirement pool exceeds Rs. 3 crore comfortably. **The Trap:** Looking at only the debt portion (PF+PPF at 8%) understates the total. The equity MF portfolio is also part of the retirement pool. **Cross-Exam Context:** Multi-component retirement corpus calculation is tested in X-A and XVII. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Break retirement planning into multiple buckets — existing corpus growth + new monthly contributions. Review annually and adjust contributions as income grows.
